title: 2007-2021 Toyota Tundra Fuel Pump Replacement Instructions
description: Replace the fuel pump on a 2007-2021 Toyota Tundra, including full tank drop, basket disassembly, pump swap, strainer replacement, and reinstallation.

items.name: Fuel pump lock ring special service tool

tips: Note that the fuel pump and the replacement procedure on the flex-fuel trucks is somewhat different and will require some additional steps.
Run the tank as low as possible before starting — 100L of fuel weighs over 160 lbs.
Wear goggles and have a rag or drain pan ready when disconnecting fuel lines, as fuel will spill out.
When lowering the tank, go slowly to avoid ripping the fuel pump wiring harness connector.
Do not rotate the pump assembly too much when removing the lock ring, as the float inside the fuel level sensor can break off.
When the lock ring tabs hit the plastic stoppers, lift each tab with a screwdriver to clear it before continuing to turn.
Replace the fuel pump strainer (Denso part #95200093) every time — do not reuse the old one.
Reuse the plastic sleeve on the pump outlet; omitting it will push the O-ring down and cause fuel pressure loss.
Always pull the old O-ring out from inside the pump housing before installing the new pump.
Lube the new pump O-ring with gasoline before sliding it on.
Blow out all dirt, dust, and debris from around the fuel pump top before opening the tank.
